metadata.project_name: Commercial Project Figure Q2 - Review/Revit/ETABS Preparation Model 0.16
notes: 0.16 keeps v0.15 preliminary sizing and updates review output readability: vertical beam labels rotate 90 degrees in DXF.
This version is the basis for Revit review generation and ETABS API adapter research.
All prior versions are retained in outputs/archive folders for comparison; do not squash version history.
Formal ETABS analysis is the next priority; YJK is future scope.

coordinate_system.grid_reference_basis: Lower-left corner of normalized podium envelope; tower footprint uses same X origin and 20m normalized depth.

traceability.source_entity_ref: shopping typical slab with atrium opening
small office tower slab, not full podium footprint

uncertainty.reason: 3-31F office floors are represented as one standard floor template to avoid repeated JSON/Revit geometry.
uncertainty.fallback_assumption: Expand only when analysis/export requires individual floors.

uncertainty.reason: Slab supported by core walls does not require duplicate beam along same wall line except for transfer/opening edge cases.
uncertainty.fallback_assumption: Keep wall as primary support line in review model.

uncertainty.reason: Mall/open-space grid uses 12m-16m spans with PT/RC band beams. This is feasible as a concept but requires formal gravity, deflection, vibration, and lateral design.
uncertainty.fallback_assumption: use v0.13 as review concept only; do not treat seed member sizes as final

keepouts.reason: No column is allowed at entrance lobby.
No column is allowed at atrium. Opening edge beams provided.
3m clear passageway at 1/F linking proposed footbridge.
0.13 keeps office/mall relatively open. Long spans are review assumptions and require formal PT/steel/composite checks before acceptance.

traceability.assumption_basis: open-space architectural intent prioritized; formal long-span design pending

issues.message: DXF generator now rotates labels for near-vertical beams by 90 degrees so transverse and longitudinal beam marks can be distinguished.
v0.14 used 1200x1200 mall columns and 650x1300 office beams without capacity/rho justification. v0.15 reduces seed sizes and documents preliminary capacity checks.
Mall edge columns use 700x700, typical mall columns 900x900, and transfer-line columns 1000x1000. Approximate axial capacity/rho checks are documented separately.
Typical office beams changed to a 450x650 slim composite/PT envelope to preserve office space; this is a structural system assumption requiring formal design.
16m mall spans cannot be accepted as ordinary RC by seed dimensions alone; PT/composite design plus deflection/vibration checks remain mandatory.
Core wall centerline beams remain omitted where the wall directly supports the slab/collector line.
This is preliminary sizing, not final design approval.
